resources.string: This stylesheet has no rules defined. Add a rule to start customizing this stylesheet.
This stylesheet rule is already defined.
Apply rule to all target elements.
All possible values have been added.
This key code is already defined.
This key code is not valid. Ensure that the key code is within the range of {c_min} to {c_max} for characters or {i_min} to {i_max} for internal special keys.
Alternatively the following links will help you to find the corresponding key code:
To find the code of a key, use the button besides the code input field. Once activated, it will record the next key press and will insert the code into the input field.
{app_name} needs to be the default keyboard to record a key code
A property with this name already exists within the current rule.
No files have been added to this extension yet. Please use the "{action_title}" action in the previous screen to import files.
{app_name} can try to leniently load the stylesheet and add missing schemas, rules, or remove invalid rules, properties, or values. Do you want {app_name} to apply these changes?
Define variables within this rule to re-use common colors or sizes in your stylesheet.

resources.string: Android requires that every custom keyboard is separately enabled before you can use it. Open the System Language & Input Settings, there enable "{app_name}".
{app_name} is now enabled in your system. To actively use it, switch to {app_name} by selecting it in the input selector dialog!

As of Android 13+, apps must ask for permission to send notifications. In Dictate Keyboard, this is only used to open a crash report screen in the event of a crash. This permission can be changed at any time in the system settings.
Dictate turns your speech into text with the help of an online AI service. To use it, you need your own personal key for such a service.
An API key is like a personal password that lets Dictate use the service for you. Creating one is free and takes about two minutes.
We recommend Groq — it\'s fast and free for everyday dictation.

1. Tap the button below to open Groq.
2. Sign in with Google or your email address.
3. Tap “Create API Key”, give it any name, and confirm.
4. Copy the key (it begins with “gsk_”).
5. Come back here and tap “Paste API key” below.

1. Tap the button below to open {provider}.
2. Create an account if you don't have one yet.
3. Create a new API key and copy it.
4. Come back here and tap “Paste API key” below.

string.name: setup__provider__open_btn
string.comment: {provider} is the provider name, e.g. Groq

resources.string: Your clipboard looks empty. Copy your key on the provider\'s page first, then tap “Paste API key” again.

string.name: setup__provider__key_detected
string.comment: {key} is the masked key, e.g. gsk_…AB12

resources.string: Advanced: pick another service instead of Groq. You\'ll need an API key from that provider.
{app_name} is ready. Switch to it with the keyboard switcher, tap the microphone, and start speaking.
You can change languages, the AI provider and everything else anytime in the settings. If you ever run into a problem or have an idea, reach out from the about screen!
Tip: you haven\'t added an AI key yet. Add one anytime in Settings → Dictate → AI providers to start dictating.

string.name: setup__floating_button__btn

string.name: setup__floating_button__title

string.name: setup__floating_button__intro
string.comment: Explanation on the optional floating-button onboarding step

resources.string: The floating button puts a small dictation bubble over any app, so you can speak into any text field without switching keyboards.

string.name: setup__floating_button__accessibility_note
string.comment: Transparency note about the accessibility service the floating button relies on

resources.string: To do this, {app_name} uses Android\'s accessibility settings — that\'s how the bubble can show over other apps and insert the transcribed text for you. The next screen guides you through enabling it.

string.name: setup__floating_button__optional_note
string.comment: Reassurance that the floating button is optional

resources.string: This is completely optional — skip it if you just want to use {app_name} as a full keyboard. You can always set it up later in the settings.

string.name: setup__floating_button__skip_btn
string.comment: Button to skip the optional floating-button step

resources.string: Sorry for the inconvenience, but Dictate has crashed due to an unexpected error.

string.name: crash_dialog__report_instructions
string.comment: Issue tracker report instructions for the crash dialog. The %s placeholder is the name of the crash report template and always in English/LTR.

resources.string: If you wish to report this error, first check out the issue tracker on GitHub if your crash has not already been reported.\nIf it hasn\'t, copy the generated crash log and open a new issue. Use the \"%s\" template and fill out the description, the steps to reproduce, and paste the generated crash log at the end. This helps in making Dictate better and more stable for everyone. Thank you!

Please enter a valid variable name matching {variable_name_regex}
Please enter a valid color string
Please enter a positive number between 0 and 100
Any value above 50% will behave as if you set 50%, consider lowering your percent size

All tasks related to importing, exporting, creating, customizing, and removing extensions can be handled through the centralized addon manager.
You can download and install extensions from the Dictate Keyboard Addons Store or import any extension file you have downloaded from the internet.

resources.string: {app_name} uses the haptic feedback interface to trigger a predefined vibration sequence for key presses. This may work exceptionally well on some devices, but completely fail or perform very poorly on others

string.name: dictate__accessibility_service_label
string.comment: Name of the Dictate accessibility service, shown in the system accessibility settings list

string.name: dictate__accessibility_service_summary
string.comment: Short one-line summary of the accessibility service in the system settings

resources.string: Show a floating button to dictate into any text field.

string.name: dictate__accessibility_service_description
string.comment: Full disclosure shown in the system accessibility settings explaining what the service does and why

resources.string: Dictate uses this service to power the floating dictation button. When enabled, it detects when a text field is focused in any app — so the button can appear — and inserts your transcribed speech into that field. Dictate only reads the focused field to place your text at the cursor; it does not collect, store or share other screen content.

string.name: dictate__overlay_notification_channel
string.comment: Name of the notification channel for the floating-button recording notification

string.name: dictate__overlay_notification_recording
string.comment: Text of the ongoing notification shown while the floating button is recording

resources.string: Recording — tap the floating button to transcribe

string.name: dictate__overlay_notification_working
string.comment: Text shown in the notification while the floating-button dictation is being transcribed

string.name: dictate__floating_button_title
string.comment: Title of the floating-button settings screen

string.name: dictate__floating_button_enable_title
string.comment: Master toggle for the floating dictation button

string.name: dictate__floating_button_enable_summary
string.comment: Summary under the floating-button master toggle

resources.string: Show a floating button to dictate into any app, even when another keyboard is active.

string.name: dictate__floating_button_permission_group
string.comment: Group title for the accessibility-permission setup

string.name: dictate__floating_button_service_title
string.comment: Row that leads to the system accessibility settings

string.name: dictate__floating_button_service_enabled
string.comment: Status when the accessibility service is on

resources.string: Enabled · tap to open system settings

string.name: dictate__floating_button_service_disabled
string.comment: Status when the accessibility service is off

resources.string: Not enabled yet · tap to set up

string.name: dictate__floating_button_disclosure_title
string.comment: Title of the prominent-disclosure dialog before enabling the accessibility service

string.name: dictate__floating_button_disclosure_message
string.comment: Prominent disclosure shown before sending the user to the accessibility settings

resources.string: To show the floating button, Dictate needs the accessibility service. It detects when a text field is focused in any app — so the button can appear — and inserts your transcribed speech into that field. Dictate only reads the focused field to place your text; it does not collect, store or share other screen content.\n\nOn the next screen, find “Dictate floating button” and turn it on.

string.name: dictate__floating_button_disclosure_continue
string.comment: Confirm button that opens the system accessibility settings

resources.string: Keep your last successful dictation so you can insert it again with the \"Re-insert\" Smartbar button — a safety net if the text field is cleared by a screen rotation or app switch. Add the button via the Smartbar editor. Stored only on this device until your next dictation.

string.comment: Accessibility label for the prompt list icon: prompt runs automatically

resources.string: Correct all spelling, grammar and punctuation mistakes in the text. Keep the original wording, tone and language as much as possible and do not add new information. Return only the corrected text, without quotation marks or explanations.

Rewrite the text so it reads more clearly and flows better, while keeping its meaning, tone and language. Do not add new information. Return only the improved text, without quotation marks or explanations.
Rewrite the text in a polite, professional and formal tone. Keep its meaning and language and add no new information. Return only the rewritten text, without quotation marks or explanations.
Rewrite the text in a warm, friendly and casual tone. Keep its meaning and language and add no new information. Return only the rewritten text, without quotation marks or explanations.
Make the text noticeably shorter and more concise while keeping all important information, its tone and its language. Return only the shortened text, without quotation marks or explanations.
Translate the text into natural, fluent English. Keep the original meaning and tone. Return only the translation, without quotation marks or explanations.
Generate a single short, original and inspiring quote. Return only the quote itself, without an author, quotation marks or any explanation.
Share one surprising and genuinely true fun fact in a single sentence. Return only the fact, without any introduction or explanation.

string.comment: Button that opens the full changelog/releases page online

string.name: changelog__intro
string.comment: Brief intro line at the top of the what's-new dialog, before the per-version notes

resources.string: Dictate has been updated. Here\'s what\'s new:

string.name: changelog__version_header
string.comment: Header shown above one release's notes. {version} is e.g. 4.0.0

string.name: changelog__donate
string.comment: Tappable invitation to donate, shown at the bottom of the what's-new dialog

resources.string: Enjoying Dictate? Tap here to support development ♥

string.name: changelog__current
string.comment: What's-new text for the 4.0.0 release. One change per line.

resources.string:
• Brand-new keyboard: Dictate now ships as a full multi-language layout keyboard with a dedicated mic key that opens the voice panel.
• Bring your own AI: pick any OpenAI-compatible provider and enter your API key under Settings → Dictate.
• Smart formatting and rewording powered by your chosen model.
• Your settings from the previous Dictate app are imported automatically.

string.name: changelog__v4_1_0
string.comment: What's-new text for the 4.1.0 release. One change per line.

resources.string:
• New: search emoji right from the keyboard.
• New transcription provider: Soniox.
• Re-insert your last dictation, and recover recordings interrupted when the keyboard closed.
• The prompt bar now refreshes live — long-press a prompt to edit it.
• The AI providers screen now shows both your active transcription and rewording providers.
• Bigger, easier-to-hit record button.
• Fixes: a support prompt could get stuck and block the bar in some languages; backup no longer fails when prompts, keyboard or theme are selected; saved API keys are kept when upgrading.

string.name: changelog__v4_2_0
string.comment: What's-new text for the 4.2.0 release. One change per line.

resources.string: • NEW: Floating dictation button — dictate straight into any app, even when another keyboard is active. Pick from three styles (Pill, Ring, Orb), watch a live waveform while you speak, drag it anywhere with edge-snapping, set its color and size, and long-press to reword. Turn it on in Settings ▸ Dictate ▸ Floating button.\n• More reliable text insertion across apps, including browsers and other keyboards.

string.name: changelog__v4_1_1
string.comment: What's-new text for the 4.1.1 release. One change per line.

resources.string: • Fix: the accent color could appear green on some devices — keys and buttons are back to the Dictate blue.
